title: Citations, Freshness, Uncertainty, Accessibility, and Feedback · Program Brief: Rag Claim Level Citations
summary: An assistant appends three source links at the bottom, hides relevance behind color, opens inaccessible PDF pages, and reports confident text without freshness or conflict state. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the claim level citations increment in accessible evidence-first answer interface. Learners progre…
objectives: Place citations at material claims and verify source ID, exact span, quotation fidelity, support, and link target.
Validate response schema, claim IDs, source IDs, quotations, spans, allowed actions, and unsafe content before display or tool use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-claim-level-citations — Place citations at material claims and verify source ID, exact span, quotation fidelity, support, and link target.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-citations-user-experience-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-citations-user-experience-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_citations_user_experience_4(claims: int = 5, claim_links: int = 5, source_links: int = 4, stale_sources: int = 0, keyboard_reachable: bool = True, status_announced: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if claims < 0 or claim_links < 0 or source_links < 0:
        return False, 0, "citation-count"
    if claim_links < claims:
        return False, claim_links, "unsupported-claim"
    if stale_sources > 0:
        return False, stale_sources, "freshness-warning"
    if not keyboard_reachable or not status_announced:
        return False, source_links, "accessible-evidence"
    assert claim_links >= claims
    return True, source_links, "inspectable-citations"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Place citations at material claims and verify source ID, exact span, quotation fidelity, support, and link target.
